title: human_escalation event payload
$comment: Provenance (WU 2.14, F2.4): authored 2026-04-23 in WU 2.9; closes finding F2.8. Fields: summary (required), reason (required, enum: spinning_detected|autonomy_requires_approval|spec_level_blocker|override_expiry_needs_review), inbox_file (optional), cycle_members (optional array of task IDs). The human_escalation event_type itself was in the Phase 1 baseline enum; this per-type payload schema is the Phase 2 WU 2.9 addition.
description: Payload shape for the `human_escalation` event appended to the feature event log when any agent raises an escalation per `shared/rules/escalation-protocol.md`. The `reason` field is the discriminator; its values are the closed set from `escalation-protocol.md` §"When to escalate". Design choice: flat schema with `cycle_members` optional across all reasons (rather than `oneOf`/`if-then-else`) — simpler at v1 while still capturing the structured-payload intent of finding F2.8. `cycle_members` is semantically reserved for `reason: spec_level_blocker` on dependency-cycle escalations and `reason: spinning_detected` on cycle-driven spinning, but is not mechanically excluded from other reasons at v1. Added in Phase 2 WU 2.9.

summary.description: One-sentence prose description of the escalation situation. Mirrors the human-readable summary in the inbox escalation file. Must be sufficient for a cold reader to understand what is blocked.

reason.description: Escalation reason. Closed enum matching the four dispatch values in `shared/rules/escalation-protocol.md` §"When to escalate". The polling loop dispatches on this field; values outside the enum are dropped to dead-letter.

inbox_file.description: Path to the escalation inbox file written under `/inbox/human-escalation/`, relative to the orchestration repo root (e.g. `inbox/human-escalation/FEAT-2026-0005-plan-review-cycle.md`). Optional but recommended for traceability — the inbox file and the event are redundant by design (see `shared/rules/escalation-protocol.md` §"Event-log accompaniment").

cycle_members.description: Task IDs involved in a dependency cycle. Populated when `reason` is `spec_level_blocker` due to a dependency cycle (or related spinning). Omit when no cycle is involved. Provides structured machine-readable data for the gap identified in finding F2.8.
